content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.6.1 / 5.6.3 WARN — Custom star UI gates App Store review by sentiment (App/RatingGateView.swift:9-20: only stars>=4 get routed to `itms-apps://…write-review`, others deflected to a feedback URL) and bypasses `SKStoreReviewController`/`requestReview`; description.txt:1 also directly solicits "rate us 5 stars!"

Pierre: Apple explicitly prohibits pre-filtering users before prompting for a review and requires using the system `requestReview` API, which itself is rating-agnostic and rate-limited — this app does neither, routing only happy users to a direct write-review link while hiding negative feedback from the Store, and the metadata explicitly asks for 5-star ratings. This is a textbook 5.6.1/5.6.3 dark pattern likely to trigger rejection. Replace this flow with `SKStoreReviewController.requestReview(in:)` (or `RequestReviewAction` in SwiftUI) shown to all users regardless of star selection, and remove the "rate us 5 stars" language from the description.
